definition: itertools and functools are standard-library modules of higher-order building blocks: itertools provides memory-efficient iterator tools (chaining, slicing, grouping, combinatorics), while functools provides tools for working with functions (caching, partial application, reduction).
layman: They're two toolboxes of ready-made helpers — itertools for looping over data efficiently, functools for shaping and speeding up functions — so you don't reinvent common patterns.
content:
## itertools & functools

These modules give you battle-tested, lazy, composable helpers instead of hand-rolled loops.

### itertools — iterator building blocks

All are **lazy** (they yield on demand, using little memory):

| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `chain(a, b)` | iterate several iterables as one stream |
| `islice(it, n)` | take a slice without materializing a list |
| `batched(it, n)` *(3.12+)* | yield fixed-size chunks |
| `groupby(it, key)` | group consecutive items by a key |
| `accumulate(it)` | running totals |
| `takewhile` / `dropwhile` | stop/skip based on a predicate |
| `count`, `cycle`, `repeat` | infinite generators |
| `product`, `combinations`, `permutations` | combinatorics |

### functools — function tools

| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `lru_cache` / `cache` | memoize a function's results |
| `partial(f, x)` | pre-fill some arguments of a function |
| `reduce(f, it)` | fold an iterable to a single value |
| `wraps` | preserve metadata when writing decorators |
| `cached_property` | compute a property once per instance |

### Why this matters for Gen AI

- **Batching**: `islice`/`batched` split thousands of documents into API-sized batches without loading everything.
- **Streaming**: `chain` merges token streams or paginated results into one sequence.
- **Caching**: `lru_cache` memoizes deterministic work (e.g. tokenizing a fixed prompt, embedding a repeated string).
- **Configured callbacks**: `partial` binds a model/temperature into a reusable call.
- **Running cost**: `accumulate` tracks cumulative token spend as a stream progresses.
keyPoints: itertools tools are lazy iterators — composable and memory-efficient.
Use islice/batched to take or chunk a stream without building big lists.
chain merges multiple iterables into one sequence.
functools.partial pre-binds arguments; reduce folds an iterable to one value.
